# Modal

A dialog/modal component for displaying content that requires user attention or interaction, overlaid on top of the main page.

## Import

```jsx
import { Modal } from "@onlinesales-ai/ui";
```

## Sub-components

| Component | Description |
| --- | --- |
| `Modal` | The root component that manages modal state. |
| `Modal.Trigger` | The element that opens the modal on click. |
| `Modal.Content` | The modal panel containing all content. |
| `Modal.Header` | The header section of the modal. |
| `Modal.Body` | The main content area of the modal. |
| `Modal.Footer` | The footer section, typically for action buttons. |
| `Modal.Title` | The title of the modal. |
| `Modal.Description` | A description or subtitle for the modal. |
| `Modal.CloseTrigger` | A button that closes the modal. |
| `Modal.ActionTrigger` | A button that performs an action and closes the modal. |

## Props

### Modal

| Prop | Type | Default | Description |
| --- | --- | --- | --- |
| `open` | `boolean` | - | Controlled open state of the modal. |
| `onOpenChange` | `(details: { open: boolean }) => void` | - | Callback when the open state changes. |
| `onClose` | `() => void` | - | Callback when the modal is closed. |
| `size` | `"xs" \| "sm" \| "md" \| "lg" \| "xl" \| "full"` | `"md"` | The size of the modal. |
| `placement` | `"center" \| "top" \| "bottom"` | `"center"` | The placement of the modal on screen. |
| `scrollBehavior` | `"inside" \| "outside"` | `"outside"` | Whether scrolling occurs inside the modal body or on the entire modal. |
| `motionPreset` | `"scale" \| "slide-in-bottom" \| "slide-in-right" \| "none"` | `"scale"` | The animation preset for opening/closing. |
| `closeOnInteractOutside` | `boolean` | `true` | Whether the modal closes when clicking outside. |
| `closeOnEscape` | `boolean` | `true` | Whether the modal closes when pressing the Escape key. |

## Usage

### Basic Modal

```jsx
<Modal>
  <Modal.Trigger asChild>
    <Button>Open Modal</Button>
  </Modal.Trigger>
  <Modal.Content>
    <Modal.Header>
      <Modal.Title>Modal Title</Modal.Title>
      <Modal.Description>This is a description of the modal.</Modal.Description>
    </Modal.Header>
    <Modal.Body>
      <p>Modal body content goes here.</p>
    </Modal.Body>
    <Modal.Footer>
      <Modal.CloseTrigger asChild>
        <Button variant="outline">Cancel</Button>
      </Modal.CloseTrigger>
      <Modal.ActionTrigger asChild>
        <Button>Confirm</Button>
      </Modal.ActionTrigger>
    </Modal.Footer>
  </Modal.Content>
</Modal>
```

### Controlled Modal

```jsx
function ControlledModal() {
  const [open, setOpen] = useState(false);

  return (
    <>
      <Button onClick={() => setOpen(true)}>Open</Button>
      <Modal open={open} onOpenChange={(e) => setOpen(e.open)}>
        <Modal.Content>
          <Modal.Header>
            <Modal.Title>Controlled Modal</Modal.Title>
          </Modal.Header>
          <Modal.Body>
            <p>This modal is controlled externally.</p>
          </Modal.Body>
          <Modal.Footer>
            <Modal.CloseTrigger asChild>
              <Button>Close</Button>
            </Modal.CloseTrigger>
          </Modal.Footer>
        </Modal.Content>
      </Modal>
    </>
  );
}
```

### Full-Screen Modal

```jsx
<Modal size="full">
  <Modal.Trigger asChild>
    <Button>Open Full Screen</Button>
  </Modal.Trigger>
  <Modal.Content>
    <Modal.Header>
      <Modal.Title>Full Screen Modal</Modal.Title>
      <Modal.CloseTrigger />
    </Modal.Header>
    <Modal.Body>
      <p>This modal takes up the entire screen.</p>
    </Modal.Body>
  </Modal.Content>
</Modal>
```
